summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates host info and environment variables to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's lifecycle script in package.json line 31 and the bundled scanner.js together form a system-info and environment exfiltration chain. The code requires os, https, fs, and child_process; collects os.hostname(), the output of `whoami` via execSync, process.cwd(), and process.env contents; and POSTs the data to the hardcoded attacker domain https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js additionally checks for and reads filesystem paths before sending them out. The package presents itself as a DeFi environment auditor, but the behavior is unconditional credential and host-identity exfiltration to a third-party-controlled GitHub Pages site, with no relationship to any legitimate auditing purpose. This fires automatically on `npm install` (lifecycle hook in package.json) and again at runtime when scanner.js is invoked.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
